text: "Died there."... This last chapter of Deuteronomy, in which the death of Moses is related, was written by Josue, or by some of the prophets.

"He buried him"... By the ministry of 
angels, and would have the place of his burial to be unknown, lest the Israelites, who were so prone to idolatry, might worship him with divine honors.
"Prince of the host of the Lord"... St. Michael, who is called prince of the people of Israel, Dan. 10:21.
"Worshipping."... Not with divine honor, but with a religious veneration of an inferior kind, suitable to the dignity of his person.
